model_description: Anthropic's fast, low-cost Claude 4.5 model with near-frontier intelligence for high-volume work.
Fast, affordable Claude. Optimized for high-volume, latency-sensitive tasks where quality-per-token matters more than raw capability.
High-speed variant of Opus 4.6. Optimized for rapid responses while retaining strong reasoning and capabilities.
Anthropic's previous-generation Opus model with 1M context, retained for downstream compatibility.
Anthropic's most capable model. Exceptional at complex reasoning, coding, creative collaboration, and scientific analysis.
Points to the latest stable Opus release. Use when you want the newest Claude without pinning a version.
Balanced Anthropic model. Strong for coding, analysis, and everyday tasks at a mid-range price point.
Anthropic's balanced Claude model with strong speed/intelligence tradeoffs, extended thinking, and 1M context.
Mistral's code-specialized model for software engineering and code completion workflows.
DeepSeek's dedicated code model. Specialized for code generation, completion, and debugging across many languages.
Groq-hosted DeepSeek R1 distilled Llama 70B reasoning model.
Fast, efficient DeepSeek variant. Optimized for high-volume applications where latency and cost are critical.
DeepSeek's flagship model. Excellent reasoning and code capabilities at a fraction of OpenAI's cost.
Previous-generation Gemini. Good balance of speed, cost, and capability for standard tasks.
Fast, efficient Gemini model. Optimized for high-volume applications with strong reasoning and code performance.
Google's flagship model. Exceptional at long-context tasks, multimodal reasoning, and code generation.
Google's smaller open-weight instruction-tuned model. Efficient for constrained deployment environments.
Google's open-weight instruction-tuned model. Strong reasoning in a smaller footprint. Good for fine-tuning and self-hosting.
Fast open-weight GLM model optimized for multilingual dialogue, tool calling, and reasoning.
Open-weight Z.AI GLM model with strong multilingual, reasoning, and instruction-following capabilities.
Higher-throughput GLM-5 Turbo model for production Z.AI workloads.
Updated GLM-5.1 model with current Z.AI pricing and long-context support.
Z.AI GLM-5 model for current-generation reasoning and agentic workloads.
Vision-capable GLM-5 Turbo variant for multimodal document, image, and video inputs.
Deprecated 16K GPT-3.5 Turbo snapshot retained for legacy downstream configurations.
Similar capabilities as text-davinci-003 but compatible with legacy Completions endpoint and not Chat Completions.
Most capable GPT-3.5 model and optimized for chat at 1/10th the cost of text-davinci-003.
Same capabilities as the standard gpt-4 mode but with 4x the context length.
Legacy GPT-4 Turbo model with a 128K context window. Kept for integrations that still route older GPT-4 workloads.
Smaller GPT-4.1 variant balancing latency, price, and strong general-purpose capability.
Lowest-cost GPT-4.1 variant for classification, extraction, and latency-sensitive tasks.
Large-context GPT-4.1 model for coding, instruction following, and multimodal document workflows.
GPT-4 successor with improved reasoning and instruction following. Strong all-around model between GPT-4o and GPT-5 series.
More capable than any GPT-3.5 model able to do more complex tasks and optimized for chat.
Smaller, faster, cheaper GPT-4o. Optimized for high-volume, cost-sensitive applications.
GPT-4 Omni — multimodal model with vision, audio, and text. Fast and cost-efficient relative to GPT-4 Turbo.
Lower-cost GPT-5 variant for high-volume tasks that still benefit from reasoning support.
Smallest GPT-5 variant for inexpensive, latency-sensitive automation.
Codex-optimized GPT-5.3 model for coding agents and long-running software engineering tasks.
GPT-5.4 optimized for image understanding and generation. Multimodal model supporting vision tasks.
Fast, cost-efficient GPT-5.4 variant for coding, computer use, and subagent workloads.
Lowest-latency GPT-5.4 variant for inexpensive high-volume tasks.
High-capability GPT-5.4 Pro model for the hardest reasoning and professional workloads.
Current OpenAI model for coding and professional work with a 1M token context window.
Most capable OpenAI model with largest context window. Optimized for complex reasoning, advanced coding, and scientific analysis.
Capable OpenAI model with large context window. Strong for complex tasks at lower cost than Pro.
General GPT-5 model for reasoning, coding, and multimodal text/image workflows.
Grok 4.2 optimized for multi-agent workflows. Enhanced tool use and coordination for parallel task execution.
xAI's flagship model. Strong reasoning with real-time web access. Known for personality and directness.
Moonshot AI's flagship model. Strong long-context understanding and reasoning at competitive pricing.
Mid-range Moonshot model. Good balance of capability and cost for standard tasks.
Groq-hosted Llama 3.1 8B model for very low-latency, low-cost chat and automation.
Groq-hosted Llama 3.3 70B model optimized for fast general-purpose chat and tool workloads.
High-speed MiniMax M2.1 variant for faster multilingual programming and agent workflows.
MiniMax M2.1 model with strong multilingual programming and enhanced coding experience.
High-speed MiniMax M2.5 variant for lower-latency coding and agent workloads.
MiniMax M2.5 model for complex programming, agent, and productivity tasks.
High-speed MiniMax M2.7 variant with the same capability profile and faster output for latency-sensitive workloads.
Beginning the journey of recursive self-improvement. Strong for programming, tool calling, search, office productivity, and complex agent workflows.
MiniMax M2 model for agentic workflows, advanced reasoning, function calling, and real-time streaming.
Mistral-7B fine-tuned on instruction datasets publicly available on HuggingFace.
Mistral-7B-v0.1 is a small yet powerful model adaptable to many use-cases.
Versioned Mistral Large 2.1 model retained for integrations pinned to the 24.11 release.
Mistral Large 3 versioned model with long context and strong multimodal/coding capabilities.
Mistral's current flagship alias for high-quality reasoning, coding, and multimodal workloads.
Mistral's balanced mid-tier model for reasoning, coding, and multimodal workflows.
Open-weight Mistral Nemo model, useful for local or low-cost multilingual deployments.
Mistral's efficient small model alias for low-cost general-purpose and agentic workloads.
NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super, an open-weight hybrid MoE model for agentic reasoning, tool use, and long-context workloads.
OpenAI reasoning model. Extended thinking model optimized for complex reasoning, science, and coding. Charges for reasoning tokens.
Lightweight OpenAI reasoning model. Fast and cost-efficient reasoning for simpler tasks.
Groq-hosted Qwen3 32B open-weight model for reasoning, code, and multilingual work.
Fast, efficient Qwen variant. Optimized for high-volume applications.
Alibaba's flagship model. Strong multilingual and reasoning capabilities with large context support.
